checks.shortDescription: An AngularJS event listener that listens for a non-existent event has no effect.
checks.title: Dead AngularJS event listener

checks.shortDescription: If the injected dependencies of a function go out of sync with its parameters, the function will become difficult to understand and maintain.
checks.title: Dependency mismatch

checks.shortDescription: Disabling strict contextual escaping (SCE) can cause security vulnerabilities.
checks.title: Disabling SCE

checks.shortDescription: Recompiling an already compiled part of the DOM can lead to unexpected behavior of directives, performance problems, and memory leaks.
checks.title: Double compilation

checks.shortDescription: Repeated dependency names are redundant for AngularJS dependency injection.
checks.title: Duplicate dependency

checks.shortDescription: Dependency-injecting a service of the wrong kind causes an error at runtime.
checks.title: Incompatible dependency injection

checks.shortDescription: URL whitelists that are too permissive can cause security vulnerabilities.
checks.title: Insecure URL whitelist

checks.shortDescription: Functions without explicit dependency injections will not work when their parameter names are minified.
checks.title: Missing explicit dependency injection

checks.shortDescription: Specifying dependency injections of an AngularJS component multiple times overrides earlier specifications.
checks.title: Repeated dependency injection

checks.shortDescription: Unused dependencies are confusing, and should be removed.
checks.title: Unused AngularJS dependency

checks.shortDescription: Using AngularJS markup in an HTML attribute that references a URL (such as 'href' or 'src') may cause the browser to send a request with an invalid URL.
checks.title: Use of AngularJS markup in URL-valued attribute

checks.shortDescription: Comments that contain commented-out code should be avoided.
checks.title: Commented-out code

checks.shortDescription: A comment that contains 'TODO' or similar keywords may indicate code that is incomplete or broken, or it may highlight an ambiguity in the software's specification.
checks.title: TODO comment

checks.shortDescription: 'alert' should not be used in production code.
checks.title: Invocation of alert

checks.shortDescription: If an HTML document contains two elements with the same id attribute, it may be interpreted differently by different browsers.
checks.title: Ambiguous HTML id attribute

checks.shortDescription: If an HTML element has two attributes with the same name but different values, its behavior may be browser-dependent.
checks.title: Conflicting HTML element attributes

checks.shortDescription: Specifying the same attribute twice on the same HTML element is redundant and may indicate a copy-paste mistake.
checks.title: Duplicate HTML element attributes

checks.shortDescription: If the id of an HTML attribute is malformed, its interpretation may be browser-dependent.
checks.title: Malformed id attribute

checks.shortDescription: DOM functions that act like 'eval' and execute strings as code are dangerous and impede program analysis and understanding.
checks.title: Call to eval-like DOM function

checks.shortDescription: External links that open in a new tab or window but do not specify link type 'noopener' or 'noreferrer' are a potential security risk.
checks.title: Potentially unsafe external link

checks.shortDescription: The special 'arguments' variable can be redefined, but this should be avoided since it makes code hard to read and maintain and may prevent compiler optimizations.
checks.title: Arguments redefined

checks.shortDescription: Assigning to a variable that is declared 'const' has either no effect or leads to a runtime error, depending on the platform.
checks.title: Assignment to constant

checks.shortDescription: If a variable is declared and initialized twice inside the same variable declaration statement, the second initialization immediately overwrites the first one.
checks.title: Conflicting variable initialization

checks.shortDescription: If two functions with the same name are declared in the same scope, one of the declarations overrides the other without warning.
checks.title: Conflicting function declarations

checks.shortDescription: An assignment to a global variable that is never used has no effect.
checks.title: Useless assignment to global variable

checks.shortDescription: An assignment to a local variable that is not used later on, or whose value is always overwritten, has no effect.
checks.title: Useless assignment to local variable

checks.shortDescription: An assignment to a property whose value is always overwritten has no effect.
checks.title: Useless assignment to property

checks.shortDescription: Variables should be declared before their first use.
checks.title: Variable not declared before use

checks.title: Default parameter references nested function

checks.shortDescription: A variable declaration statement that declares the same variable twice is confusing and hard to maintain.
checks.title: Duplicate variable declaration

checks.shortDescription: Omitting the name of a parameter causes its type annotation to be parsed as the name.
checks.title: Ineffective parameter type

checks.shortDescription: Referencing an undeclared global variable in a class that has a member of the same name is confusing and may indicate a bug.
checks.title: Missing 'this' qualifier

checks.shortDescription: If a variable is not declared as a local variable, it becomes a global variable by default, which may be unintentional and could lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Missing variable declaration

checks.shortDescription: A reference to a static method from within an instance method needs to be qualified with the class name, not `this`.
checks.title: Wrong use of 'this' for static method

checks.shortDescription: Declaring the same variable twice is confusing and may even suggest a latent bug.
checks.title: Redeclared variable

checks.shortDescription: A method declaration with a name that is a special keyword in another context is suspicious.
checks.title: Suspicious method name declaration

checks.shortDescription: Accessing a let-bound variable before its declaration will lead to a runtime error on ECMAScript 2015-compatible platforms.
checks.title: Access to let-bound variable in temporal dead zone

checks.shortDescription: Functions with many parameters are hard to read and hard to use.
checks.title: Function with too many parameters

checks.shortDescription: If a function has two parameters with the same name, the second parameter shadows the first one, which makes the code hard to understand and error-prone.
checks.title: Duplicate parameter names

checks.shortDescription: If an object literal has two properties with the same name, the second property overwrites the first one, which makes the code hard to understand and error-prone.
checks.title: Overwritten property

checks.shortDescription: Having multiple overloads with the same parameter types in TypeScript makes all overloads except the first one unreachable, as the compiler always resolves calls to the textually...
checks.title: Unreachable method overloads

checks.shortDescription: If the top-level of a module accesses a variable from a cyclic import, its value depends on which module is globally imported first.
checks.title: Unstable cyclic import

checks.shortDescription: Unused parameters make functions hard to read and hard to use, and should be removed.
checks.title: Unused parameter

checks.shortDescription: Unused properties may be a symptom of a bug and should be examined carefully.
checks.title: Unused property

checks.shortDescription: Unused variables, imports, functions or classes may be a symptom of a bug and should be examined carefully.
checks.title: Unused variable, import, function or class

checks.title: Enabling Electron allowRunningInsecureContent

checks.shortDescription: Disabling webSecurity can cause critical security vulnerabilities.
checks.title: Disabling Electron webSecurity

checks.title: Enabling Node.js integration for Electron web content renderers

checks.shortDescription: Checking the sign of the result of a bitwise operation may yield unexpected results.
checks.title: Sign check of bitwise operation

checks.shortDescription: If the same expression occurs on both sides of a comparison operator, the operator is redundant, and probably indicates a mistake.
checks.title: Comparison of identical values

checks.shortDescription: Arithmetic comparisons with NaN are useless: nothing is considered to be equal to NaN, not even NaN itself, and similarly nothing is considered greater or less than NaN.
checks.title: Comparison with NaN

checks.shortDescription: If two conditions in an 'if'-'else if' chain are identical, the second condition will never hold.
checks.title: Duplicate 'if' condition

checks.shortDescription: Listing the same property twice in one object literal is redundant and may indicate a copy-paste mistake.
checks.title: Duplicate property

checks.shortDescription: If two cases in a switch statement have the same label, the second case will never be executed.
checks.title: Duplicate switch case

checks.shortDescription: An expression that has no effect and is used in a void context is most likely redundant and may indicate a bug.
checks.title: Expression has no effect

checks.shortDescription: An equality comparison between two values that cannot be meaningfully converted to the same type will always yield 'false', and an inequality comparison will always yield 'true'.
checks.title: Comparison between inconvertible types

checks.shortDescription: Relying on implicit conversion of operands is error-prone and makes code hard to read.
checks.title: Implicit operand conversion

checks.shortDescription: Using a promise without awaiting its result can lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Missing await

checks.shortDescription: Two variables are being compared using a relational operator, but one is also used to index into the other, suggesting a ".length" is missing from the comparison.
checks.title: Missing '.length' in comparison

checks.shortDescription: Joining constant strings into a longer string where two words are concatenated without a separating space usually indicates a text error.
checks.title: Missing space in string concatenation

checks.shortDescription: Misspelled identifiers make code harder to read and understand.
checks.title: Misspelled identifier

checks.shortDescription: Misspelling a variable name implicitly introduces a global variable, which may not lead to a runtime error, but is likely to give wrong results.
checks.title: Misspelled variable name

checks.shortDescription: Passing identical, or seemingly identical, operands to an operator such as subtraction or conjunction may indicate a typo; even if it is intentional, it makes the code hard to...
checks.title: Identical operands

checks.shortDescription: Assigning a variable to itself has no effect.
checks.title: Self assignment

checks.shortDescription: The integer shift operators '<<', '>>' and '>>>' only take the five least significant bits of their right operand into account.
checks.title: Shift out of range

checks.title: String instead of regular expression

checks.shortDescription: Trying to invoke a value that is not a function will result in a runtime exception.
checks.title: Invocation of non-function

checks.shortDescription: Trying to access a property of "null" or "undefined" will result in a runtime exception.
checks.title: Property access on null or undefined

checks.shortDescription: Invoking an event handler method as a function can cause a runtime error.
checks.title: Unbound event handler receiver

checks.shortDescription: Nested expressions involving binary bitwise operators and comparisons are easy to misunderstand without additional disambiguating parentheses or whitespace.
checks.title: Unclear precedence of nested operators

checks.shortDescription: An unknown directive has no effect and may indicate a misspelling.
checks.title: Unknown directive

checks.shortDescription: Defensive code that guards against a situation that never happens is not needed.
checks.title: Unneeded defensive code

checks.shortDescription: Nested expressions where the formatting contradicts the grouping enforced by operator precedence are difficult to read and may even indicate a bug.
checks.title: Whitespace contradicts operator precedence

checks.shortDescription: A 'param' tag that does not include a name is confusing since it is unclear which parameter it is meant to document.
checks.title: Bad param tag

checks.shortDescription: A JSDoc 'param' tag that refers to a non-existent parameter is confusing and may indicate badly maintained code.
checks.title: JSDoc tag for non-existent parameter

checks.title: Undocumented parameter

checks.title: Use of arguments.caller or arguments.callee

checks.title: Useless type test

checks.title: Conditional comments

checks.title: Use of debugger statement

checks.shortDescription: The operand of the 'delete' operator should always be a property accessor.

checks.shortDescription: Omitted elements in array literals are easy to miss and should not be used.

checks.shortDescription: The 'eval' function and the 'Function' constructor execute strings as code.

checks.shortDescription: Non-standard language features such as expression closures or let expressions make it harder to reuse code.
checks.title: Use of platform-specific language features

checks.title: Use of incompletely initialized object

checks.title: Unused index variable

checks.title: Regular expression always matches

checks.shortDescription: Using externally controlled strings to construct code may allow a malicious user to execute arbitrary code.

checks.title: Bad HTML filtering regexp

checks.title: Host header poisoning in email generation

checks.shortDescription: Building an XPath expression from user-controlled sources is vulnerable to insertion of malicious code by the user.
checks.title: XPath injection

checks.shortDescription: The Helmet middleware is used to set security-related HTTP headers in Express applications.
checks.title: Insecure configuration of Helmet security middleware

checks.shortDescription: User input should not be used in regular expressions without first being escaped, otherwise a malicious user may be able to inject an expression that could require exponential...
checks.title: Regular expression injection

checks.shortDescription: A server that can be forced to crash may be vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks.
checks.title: Server crash

checks.shortDescription: Calling a method with a user-controlled name may dispatch to an unexpected target, which could cause an exception.
checks.title: Unvalidated dynamic method call

checks.shortDescription: An HTTP request handler that performs expensive operations without restricting the rate at which operations can be carried out is vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks.
checks.title: Missing rate limiting

checks.shortDescription: Allocating objects or timers with user-controlled sizes or durations can cause resource exhaustion.
checks.title: Resource exhaustion

checks.shortDescription: Parsing user input as an XML document with arbitrary internal entity expansion is vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks.
checks.title: XML internal entity expansion

checks.shortDescription: Hard-coding credentials in source code may enable an attacker to gain unauthorized access.
checks.title: Hard-coded credentials

checks.shortDescription: Conditions that the user controls are not suited for making security-related decisions.
checks.title: User-controlled bypass of security check

checks.shortDescription: Comparing different kinds of HTTP request data may be a symptom of an insufficient security check.
checks.title: Comparison of user-controlled data of different kinds

checks.shortDescription: Downloading executables and other sensitive files over an insecure connection opens up for potential man-in-the-middle attacks.
checks.title: Download of sensitive file through insecure connection

checks.shortDescription: Using a resource from an untrusted or compromised domain makes your code vulnerable to receiving malicious code.
checks.title: Untrusted domain used in script or other content

checks.shortDescription: Including functionality from an untrusted source may allow an attacker to control the functionality and execute arbitrary code.
checks.title: Inclusion of functionality from an untrusted source

checks.shortDescription: Iterating over an object with a user-controlled .length property can cause indefinite looping.
checks.title: Loop bound injection

checks.shortDescription: Sanitizing an HTTP request parameter may be ineffective if the user controls its type.
checks.title: Type confusion through parameter tampering

checks.shortDescription: Failing to set a password reduces the security of your code.
checks.title: Empty password in configuration file

checks.shortDescription: Writing network data directly to the file system allows arbitrary file upload and might indicate a backdoor.
checks.title: Network data written to file

checks.shortDescription: Modifying an object obtained via a user-controlled property name may lead to accidental mutation of the built-in Object prototype, and possibly escalate to remote code execution...
checks.title: Prototype-polluting assignment

checks.shortDescription: Functions recursively assigning properties on objects may be the cause of accidental modification of a built-in prototype object.
checks.title: Prototype-polluting function

checks.shortDescription: Recursively merging a user-controlled object into another object can allow an attacker to modify the built-in Object prototype, and possibly escalate to remote code execution or...
checks.title: Prototype-polluting merge call

checks.shortDescription: Creating a hash of a password with low computational effort makes the hash vulnerable to password cracking attacks.
checks.title: Use of password hash with insufficient computational effort

checks.shortDescription: Making a client-to-server request with user-controlled data in the URL allows a request forgery attack against the client.
checks.title: Client-side request forgery

checks.shortDescription: Making a network request with user-controlled data in the URL allows for request forgery attacks.
checks.title: Server-side request forgery

checks.shortDescription: Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) policy allows overly broad access.
checks.title: Permissive CORS configuration

checks.shortDescription: The 'else' clause of an 'if' statement should be aligned with the 'if' it belongs to.
checks.title: Misleading indentation of dangling 'else'

checks.shortDescription: A loop that executes at most once is confusing and should be rewritten as a conditional.
checks.title: Loop body executes at most once

checks.shortDescription: Ignoring the result of an array method that does not modify its receiver is generally an error.
checks.title: Ignoring result from pure array method

checks.shortDescription: Functions where some execution paths return an explicit value while others "fall off" the end of the function and return 'undefined' are hard to maintain and use.
checks.title: Not all control paths return a value

checks.shortDescription: A 'for' loop that increments its loop variable but checks it against a lower bound, or decrements its loop variable but checks it against an upper bound, will either stop...
checks.title: Inconsistent direction of for loop

checks.shortDescription: A function should either always return an explicit value, or never return a value.
checks.title: Inconsistent return statements

checks.shortDescription: A non-case label appearing in a switch statement that is textually aligned with a case label is confusing to read, or may even indicate a bug.
checks.title: Non-case label in switch statement

checks.shortDescription: Removing elements from an array while iterating over it can cause the loop to skip over some elements, unless the loop index is decremented accordingly.
checks.title: Loop iteration skipped due to shifting

checks.shortDescription: The body of a control statement should have appropriate indentation to clarify which statements it controls and which ones it does not control.
checks.title: Misleading indentation after control statement

checks.shortDescription: Nested loops in which the iteration variable is the same for each loop are difficult to understand.
checks.title: Nested loops with same variable

checks.shortDescription: An assignment to a local variable in a return statement is useless, since the variable will immediately go out of scope and its value is lost.
checks.title: Return statement assigns local variable

checks.shortDescription: A 'return' statement appearing outside a function will cause an error when it is executed.
checks.title: Return statement outside function

checks.shortDescription: A loop iteration variable is unused, which suggests an error.
checks.title: Unused loop iteration variable

checks.shortDescription: Unreachable statements are often indicative of missing code or latent bugs and should be avoided.
checks.title: Unreachable statement

checks.shortDescription: Using the return value of a function that does not return an expression is indicative of a mistake.
checks.title: Use of returnless function

checks.shortDescription: A comparison that always evaluates to true or always evaluates to false may indicate faulty logic and dead code.
checks.title: Useless comparison test

checks.shortDescription: If a conditional expression always evaluates to true or always evaluates to false, this suggests incomplete code or a logic error.
checks.title: Useless conditional

checks.shortDescription: An arrow method on a Vue instance doesn't have its `this` variable bound to the Vue instance.
checks.title: Arrow method on Vue instance
